frontierComparison.interpretation: The exact rank partition expands the visible frontier by one open finite token, but it also replaces singleton q-child ambiguity with one whole 24-bucket q-avoiding batch-cover obligation.

assembledPieces.claim: The 22-bucket successor q-avoiding cover was already assembled and routed to the whole 24-bucket post-successor rank surface.
The deterministic rank-boundary packet accounts for every post-successor bucket q in {149,151,157,163,167,173,179,181,191,193,197,199,211,223,227,229,233,239,241,251,257,263,269,277}.
Every source row contributes exactly two post-successor roots, yielding 222,345,036,453,733,797,142,322,640,306 root children.
The remaining finite surface is the whole 24-bucket post-successor q-avoiding class set of size 5,058,399,114,142,580,922,880,572,195,875,967.

supportedClaim: The post-successor 24-bucket rank partition is fully accounted as a finite theorem-facing boundary, so the only allowed next selector-shaped work is a whole-boundary q-avoiding batch cover, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact survivor boundary over all 24 buckets.

compressionCandidate.statement: Reuse the row-uniform CRT/root-count machinery from the 17-, 20-, and 22-bucket q-avoiding covers, starting strictly after each row's post-successor obstruction prime.
compressionCandidate.whyItMightCollapseTheRepeatedWork: The source packet preserves all 718 row classifications, q-avoiding counts, and post-successor obstruction primes; if every row again has a first later square obstruction, the whole 24-bucket q-avoiding surface can close without enumerating all classes.
compressionCandidate.failureBoundary: If the row-uniform cover no longer closes, emit the deterministic survivor boundary grouped by post-successor prime and previous successor/post-next/next/later-prime support.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.openBoundary: The 5,058,399,114,142,580,922,880,572,195,875,967 post-successor q-avoiding classes are not yet covered or structurally decomposed.
remainingPuzzleBoundary.outsideClaim: This packet does not close post-successor root children, post-successor q-avoiding classes, the p479 unavailable complement, q97, p443 unavailable, the wider p4217 complement, collision-free matching, or Problem 848.

oneNextAction.action: Run the whole-boundary 24-bucket post-successor q-avoiding batch cover, or emit a deterministic exact survivor/rank boundary if it does not close.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If no whole-boundary cover exists, write the deterministic survivor boundary grouped by post-successor prime and prior support; do not open q149/q151 singleton descent first.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The entire 24-bucket surface has already been partitioned exactly and the previous q-avoiding covers closed row-uniformly, so a single whole-boundary batch attempt is more informative than any singleton q child.
oneNextAction.completionRule: Every one of the 24 post-successor q-avoiding buckets is covered, structurally decomposed, or listed in a deterministic exact survivor boundary before singleton descent.

nextTheoremMove: Run the whole 24-bucket post-successor q-avoiding batch cover using the deterministic rank boundary; if it does not close, emit an exact survivor/rank boundary over all 24 buckets before singleton q-child descent.
